ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the data point */,
  "cnt" INTEGER /* Count of weather observations */,
  "t1" TEXT /* Temperature 1 (in degrees Celsius) */,
  "t2" TEXT /* Temperature 2 (in degrees Celsius) */,
  "hum" TEXT /* Humidity (in percentage) */,
  "wind_speed" TEXT /* Wind speed (in km/h) */,
  "weather_code" INTEGER /* Weather code (e.g., clear, cloudy, rainy) */,
  "is_holiday" INTEGER /* Is it a holiday (0 = no, 1 = yes) */,
  "is_weekend" INTEGER /* Is it a weekend (0 = no, 1 = yes) */,
  "season" INTEGER /* Season (1 = Spring, 2 = Summer, 3 = Autumn, 4 = Winter) */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "wind_direction" TEXT /* Wind direction (e.g., N, S, E, W) */,
  "rainfall" TEXT /* Rainfall (in mm) */,
  "snowfall" TEXT /* Snowfall (in cm) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_season FOREIGN KEY ("season") REFERENCES seasons ("season_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_weather_code FOREIGN KEY ("weather_code") REFERENCES weather_codes ("weather_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_is_holiday FOREIGN KEY ("is_holiday") REFERENCES holidays ("holiday_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Longitude of the location */,
  "elevation" TEXT /* Elevation of the location (in meters) */,
  "city" TEXT /* City name */,
  "state" TEXT /* State name */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country name */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the station is located */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "station_type" TEXT /* Type of the weather station (e.g., airport, city, rural) */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date of installation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_codes" (
  "weather_code" INTEGER /* Weather code */,
  "weather_description" TEXT /* Description of the weather code */,
  "weather_category" TEXT /* Category of the weather code (e.g., sunny, cloudy, rainy) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "seasons" (
  "season_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each season */,
  "season_name" TEXT /* Name of the season */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the season */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the season */,
  "season_description" TEXT /* Description of the season */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("season_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "holidays" (
  "holiday_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each holiday */,
  "holiday_name" TEXT /* Name of the holiday */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the holiday */,
  "holiday_description" TEXT /* Description of the holiday */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("holiday_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point */,
  "quality_flag" INTEGER /* Quality flag (0 = good, 1 = bad) */,
  "quality_description" TEXT /* Description of the data quality */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the data source (e.g., weather station, satellite, radar) */,
  "source_description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_description" TEXT /* Description of the access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine learning model making the prediction */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data used for the prediction */,
  "prediction_date" TEXT /* Date when the prediction was made */,
  "prediction_value" TEXT /* Value of the prediction */,
  "prediction_confidence" TEXT /* Confidence of the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES machine_learning_models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "machine_learning_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of the model (e.g., linear regression, decision tree) */,
  "model_description" TEXT /* Description of the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being evaluated */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., accuracy, precision) */,
  "metric_value" TEXT /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "metric_description" TEXT /* Description of the performance metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_performance_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES machine_learning_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message of the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, SMS, push notification) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notification_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, SMS, push notification) */,
  "notification_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of notifications (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notification_settings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date of the forecast */,
  "forecast_value" TEXT /* Value of the forecast */,
  "forecast_confidence" TEXT /* Confidence of the forecast */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_training_data" (
  "training_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each training event */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being trained */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data used for training */,
  "training_date" TEXT /* Date when the model was trained */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("training_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_training_data_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_training_data_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES machine_learning_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_deployment" (
  "deployment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each deployment */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being deployed */,
  "deployment_date" TEXT /* Date when the model was deployed */,
  "deployment_environment" TEXT /* Environment where the model was deployed (e.g., production, staging) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("deployment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_deployment_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES machine_learning_models ("model_id")
)
